name.rationale: Simple string identifier with no security implications. Cannot be used to access resources or execute code.

location.rationale: Geographic/regional identifier only. Determines where the resource is stored but has no access control implications.

description.description: Description of the `CustomTargetType`. Max length is 255 characters.
description.rationale: Fext field for documentation. Doesn't affect system behaviour or grant access.

annotations.description: User annotations. These attributes can only be set and used by the user, and not by Cloud Deploy. See https://google.aip.dev/128#annotations for more details such as format and size limitations. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the annotations present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_annotations` for all of the annotations present on the resource.
annotations.rationale: Key-value metadata for user documentation only. Explicitly stated as 'can only be set and used by the user, and not by Cloud Deploy' - no operational impact.

labels.description: Labels are attributes that can be set and used by both the user and by Cloud Deploy. Labels must meet the following constraints: * Keys and values can contain only lowercase letters, numeric characters, underscores, and dashes. * All characters must use UTF-8 encoding, and international characters are allowed. * Keys must start with a lowercase letter or international character. * Each resource is limited to a maximum of 64 labels. Both keys and values are additionally constrained to be <= 128 bytes. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.
labels.rationale: Tags that could theoretically be used for resource selection, but in this context they're just organizational markers with no execution or access control

custom_actions.description: Configures render and deploy for the `CustomTargetType` using Skaffold custom actions. Structure is [documented below](#nested_custom_actions).
custom_actions.rationale: Executes arbitrary code through Skaffold actions and can pull code from external sources (Git repos, Cloud Storage, Cloud Build repos), creating supply chain attack vectors and code execution risks.

render_action.description: The Skaffold custom action responsible for render operations. If not provided then Cloud Deploy will perform the render operations via `skaffold render`.
render_action.rationale: Can execute arbitrary code during render phase. Should be restricted to approved render actions only.

deploy_action.description: The Skaffold custom action responsible for deploy operations.
deploy_action.rationale: Can execute arbitrary code during deploy phase with potentially elevated privileges. Critical security boundary.

include_skaffold_modules.description: List of Skaffold modules Cloud Deploy will include in the Skaffold Config as required before performing diagnose. Structure is [documented below](#nested_custom_actions_include_skaffold_modules).
include_skaffold_modules.rationale: Can include external Skaffold configurations that may contain malicious code or reference untrusted sources.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
project.rationale: Specifies resource ownership/billing scope. Access is already controlled by Google Cloud IAM at the project level.

include_skaffold_modules.description: List of Skaffold modules Cloud Deploy will include in the Skaffold Config as required before performing diagnose.
include_skaffold_modules.rationale: Parent block for external module inclusion - inherits security concerns from child blocks.

configs.description: The Skaffold Config modules to use from the specified source.
configs.rationale: Simple list of configuration names - no direct security impact as it doesn't specify sources.

git.description: Remote git repository containing the Skaffold Config modules. Structure is [documented below](#nested_custom_actions_include_skaffold_modules_include_skaffold_modules_git).
git.rationale: References external Git repositories which could contain malicious Skaffold configurations or be compromised.

google_cloud_storage.description: Cloud Storage bucket containing Skaffold Config modules. Structure is [documented below](#nested_custom_actions_include_skaffold_modules_include_skaffold_modules_google_cloud_storage).
google_cloud_storage.rationale: References external Cloud Storage buckets which could contain malicious configurations or be compromised.

google_cloud_build_repo.description: Cloud Build 2nd gen repository containing the Skaffold Config modules. Structure is [documented below](#nested_custom_actions_include_skaffold_modules_include_skaffold_modules_google_cloud_build_repo).
google_cloud_build_repo.rationale: References external Cloud Build repositories which could contain malicious configurations or be compromised.

repo.description: Git repository the package should be cloned from.

path.description: Relative path from the repository root to the Skaffold file.

ref.description: Git ref the package should be cloned from.

source.description: Cloud Storage source paths to copy recursively. For example, providing `gs://my-bucket/dir/configs/*` will result in Skaffold copying all files within the `dir/configs` directory in the bucket `my-bucket`.

path.description: Relative path from the source to the Skaffold file.

repository.description: Cloud Build 2nd gen repository in the format of 'projects/<project>/locations/<location>/connections/<connection>/repositories/<repository>'.

path.description: Relative path from the repository root to the Skaffold file.

ref.description: Branch or tag to use when cloning the repository.
